prior.title: Alabama ADEM Construction Stormwater Permits - Multiple Facilities in Mobile County
prior.summary: Multiple construction stormwater permits (ALR10C80C, ALR10C80B, ALR10C80D) issued by Alabama Department of Environmental Management for facilities in Mobile County, including F3 LLC, Coastal Ready Mix LLC, and Mobile County Commission. Permits relate to water management and construction stormwater control.
prior.full_brief:
# Purpose and Scope of This Document

This document covers Alabama ADEM Construction General Permit No. ALR100000 as it applies to construction stormwater discharges at multiple facilities located in Mobile County, Alabama. The April 1, 2026 permit update is the operative version as of the date of this brief. The document addresses Notice of Intent obligations, Construction Best Management Practices Plan requirements, Qualified Credentialed Professional roles, the Mobile County MS4 Phase II overlay, and the dual-layer compliance structure that applies when construction activity discharges into the Mobile County municipal separate storm sewer system.

**Convention notes:** All binding-obligation language is either a verbatim FACT grounded to a source block or is labeled as an analytical inference, operational implication, or legal confirmation required. This document does not constitute legal advice. Items requiring legal review are flagged explicitly. The workspace is referenced by operational profile, never by name.

**Date of this brief:** 2025-07-14.

---

# What This Regulation Is and Why It Applies to the Workspace

## The Regulation

The Alabama Construction General Permit (CGP), permit number ALR100000, is an NPDES general permit issued by the Alabama Department of Environmental Management (ADEM) under the authority of the federal Clean Water Act. The CGP "allows for the discharge of stormwater associated with land-disturbance activities when appropriate measures are put in place to reduce the effects of erosion in stormwater runoff and preserve water quality in the State of Alabama." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

The permit covers "construction activity, for the purpose of the CGP," which "is defined as the disruption of soils associated with clearing, grading, excavating, filling of land, or other similar activities which may result in soil erosion." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

ADEM established the General NPDES Permit No. ALR100000 for "discharges associated with regulated construction activity that will result in land disturbance equal to or greater than one acre or from construction activities involving less than one acre and which are part of a common plan of development or sale equal to or greater than one acre," effective April 1, 2011. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

The 2026 version of the permit took effect April 1, 2026. The permit table in the ADEM NPDES permits page lists ALR100000 (Construction) with an expiration date of 03/31/2031. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits

## Why It Applies to the Workspace

For workspaces operating as construction site owners or operators — including those managing multiple facilities in Mobile County — the CGP is the operative stormwater discharge authorization framework. The one-acre threshold and the common-plan-of-development rule mean that even individual sites smaller than one acre may fall within CGP coverage if they are part of a larger coordinated development plan. *Operational implication:* For workspaces with multiple parcels or phased construction in Mobile County, the common-plan-of-development provision creates a coverage obligation that aggregates across sites, not just per individual footprint.

Mobile County is designated by EPA and ADEM as an owner/operator of a Phase II municipal separate storm sewer system (MS4). This designation creates a dual-layer compliance structure: construction site operators must comply with the CGP directly, and they also discharge into a system that is itself regulated under a separate MS4 permit. *Source: Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, accessed 2025. https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/

*Analytical inference:* This dual-layer structure means Mobile County construction sites face both the state CGP obligations administered by ADEM and local construction-site-runoff-control requirements that Mobile County is obligated to enforce under its own MS4 permit. Sites that are compliant with the CGP may still be subject to Mobile County inspection and enforcement under the MS4 program's Construction Site Runoff Control minimum control measure.

---

# Issues Requiring Immediate Action

## COST ALERT — Confirm Permit Coverage Under the April 1, 2026 CGP

The 2026 CGP is now in effect. The ADEM CGP program page states: "The 2021 CGP has EXPIRED. The 2026 CGP is now in effect." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

*Operational implication:* Workspaces holding coverage under the 2021 permit must confirm whether their existing coverage automatically transferred to the 2026 permit or whether a new or updated Notice of Intent is required under the 2026 permit terms. Operating under an expired permit constitutes unpermitted discharge. *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether existing 2021-vintage NOI coverage auto-converted to the 2026 permit without action, or whether re-filing under the 2026 permit is required for each active Mobile County site.

## ACTION REQUIRED — Electronic NOI Submission for All Active and New Sites

"In accordance with ADEM Admin. Code r. 335-6-1-.04 Electronic Reporting Requirements, as of December 21, 2020, Notices of Intent (NOIs) to discharge under a General Permit must be submitted electronically." *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits

*Operational implication:* Any active Mobile County construction site that has not filed an electronic NOI through the Alabama Environmental Permitting and Compliance System (AEPACS) is out of compliance with the electronic-reporting requirement that has been in effect since December 21, 2020. Workspaces with legacy paper NOI filings must audit each site immediately.

## ACTION REQUIRED — Verify QCP-Signed CBMPP is Current for Each Site

"Operators / owners of all regulated construction sites must implement and maintain effective erosion and sediment controls in accordance with a Construction Best Management Practices Plan (CBMPP) prepared and certified by a Qualified Credentialed Professional (QCP)." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

*Operational implication:* With the 2026 permit now in force, each Mobile County site's CBMPP must be reviewed against any new 2026 permit requirements. A CBMPP prepared under the 2021 permit that has not been updated to reflect 2026 conditions may be deficient. Each site's QCP must certify the current CBMPP version.

## MONITORING — Mobile County MS4 Permit Renewal

The Mobile County Phase II Stormwater NPDES permit (ALR040000) expires September 30, 2026. The permit table entry reads "ALR040000 (PHASE II MS4) 09/30/2026." *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits The Mobile County page states "The County's current Phase II Stormwater NPDES permit was effective on October 1, 2021 and will expire on September 30, 2026." *Source: Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, accessed 2025. https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/

*Analytical inference:* A renewed MS4 permit may impose updated or more stringent local construction site runoff control requirements on Mobile County, which would cascade into additional compliance obligations for construction operators within the county. Re-check window: Q4 2026 when renewal terms are published.

---

# How the Workspace Sits in the Compliance Chain

## Construction Site Operator / Owner Role

The CGP places primary compliance obligations on "construction site operators / owners seeking coverage under this general permit," who "must submit a Notice of Intent (NOI) in accordance with the permit requirements." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

*Operational implication:* For workspaces that own or control the land being disturbed, the operator/owner role is straightforward and the CGP obligations attach directly. For workspaces acting as general contractors on land owned by a client, *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether the workspace, as the party exercising operational control over construction activities, is the "operator" for CGP purposes, and what co-permittee obligations, if any, attach to the property owner.

## Mobile County MS4 Operator Overlay

Mobile County, as the MS4 owner/operator designated by EPA and ADEM, is "required to reduce the amount of stormwater pollution entering our waters." *Source: Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, accessed 2025. https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/ The MS4 program's Construction Site Runoff Control minimum control measure creates an enforcement layer at the county level. *Operational implication:* Mobile County may inspect construction sites independently of ADEM under its MS4 permit obligations. The workspace's compliance chain therefore includes both the state CGP regulator (ADEM) and the local MS4 operator (Mobile County).

## QCP Role in the Chain

The CBMPP must be "prepared and certified by a Qualified Credentialed Professional (QCP)." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether the workspace's in-house personnel hold QCP credentials sufficient to self-certify CBMPPs, or whether an independent QCP engagement is required for each Mobile County site.

---

# Authoritative Guidance Document Analysis

## 2026 Permit Changes Document

The ADEM CGP program page references an "ALR100000 – 2026 Permit Changes" document and a PowerPoint version, both linked from the CGP page. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit The substantive content of the 2026 changes document is not reproduced in the source blocks provided for this brief.

*Specific content of the 2026 Permit Changes document not available from primary sources as of 2025-07-14.* The document exists and is linked from the ADEM CGP page, but its full text was not included in the source blocks. *Operational implication:* Workspaces managing active Mobile County sites should retrieve and review this document as the definitive statement of what changed from the 2021 to the 2026 permit. The ADEM CGP program page URL is the authoritative retrieval point: https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

## Alabama Handbook for Erosion Control

The CGP program page references the "Alabama Handbook for Erosion Control" as a supporting technical document, linked from the same page. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit The handbook's specific BMP specifications and design standards are not reproduced in the source blocks. *Operational implication:* The handbook provides the technical baseline for CBMPP preparation; workspaces should confirm their QCP is using the current edition consistent with the 2026 permit.

## CBMPP Template

ADEM provides a CBMPP template linked from the CGP program page. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit The template's specific content is not reproduced in the source blocks. *Operational implication:* Use of the ADEM-provided template is a baseline risk-reduction measure; workspaces with multiple sites should confirm each site's CBMPP uses or is consistent with the current 2026-era template.

---

# Anticipated Authoritative Guidance and Pending Regulatory Events

## Mobile County MS4 Permit Renewal — MONITORING

The Mobile County Phase II MS4 NPDES permit (ALR040000) expires September 30, 2026. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits The renewal process will be administered by ADEM. The renewed permit will set the Construction Site Runoff Control requirements that Mobile County enforces over construction operators within the county for the next permit cycle.

*Analytical inference:* Based on standard NPDES renewal cycles, a renewed MS4 permit is typically issued within 6–18 months of expiration; a draft permit and public comment period may precede final issuance. Workspaces with long-duration Mobile County construction projects should monitor the ADEM public notices page and the Mobile County stormwater program page for draft permit publication. Re-check window: October 2026.

## ADEM Environmental Management Commission Rulemaking — MONITORING

The ADEM website lists a "Division 1 & 6 Rulemaking" event scheduled for July 7, 2026. *Source: ADEM Home Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/ The subject matter of this rulemaking is not specified in the source blocks. *Analytical inference:* Division 1 covers administrative and permit fee rules; Division 6 covers water quality standards. Changes to either could affect CGP fee schedules or the water quality standards that drive permit conditions. Workspaces should monitor the July 7, 2026 rulemaking outcome for any CGP-relevant provisions.

---

# Threshold Questions

## Question 1: Does the one-acre threshold apply per site or across a common plan of development?

**Decided.** The CGP covers "land disturbance equal to or greater than one acre or from construction activities involving less than one acre and which are part of a common plan of development or sale equal to or greater than one acre." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit *Operational implication:* For workspaces with multiple Mobile County sites that are part of a single development plan, the aggregation rule applies regardless of whether each individual parcel exceeds one acre.

## Question 2: Is the 2026 CGP the operative permit for all active sites?

**Decided.** "The 2021 CGP has EXPIRED. The 2026 CGP is now in effect." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit All currently active sites are governed by the April 1, 2026 permit.

## Question 3: Is electronic NOI submission mandatory?

**Decided.** "As of December 21, 2020, Notices of Intent (NOIs) to discharge under a General Permit must be submitted electronically" under ADEM Admin. Code r. 335-6-1-.04. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits Paper NOI submission is not a compliant option for new or updated filings.

## Question 4: Does the workspace qualify as an "operator" under the CGP?

*Legal Confirmation Required:* The CGP addresses "construction site operators / owners," but the specific definition of "operator" and how it applies to a workspace acting as general contractor versus property owner, or managing sites on behalf of clients, requires review of the permit text and Alabama case law or ADEM guidance. This determination drives who bears primary CGP coverage obligations on each Mobile County site.

## Question 5: Do Mobile County's MS4 Construction Site Runoff Control obligations impose requirements beyond the CGP?

*Legal Confirmation Required:* The MS4 program creates a Construction Site Runoff Control minimum control measure that Mobile County enforces. Whether local ordinances or program requirements impose obligations on construction operators beyond the state CGP requires review of the current Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan and any local ordinances adopted under it.

---

# Substantive Requirements

## Notice of Intent (NOI) — Pre-Construction

"Construction site operators / owners seeking coverage under this general permit must submit a Notice of Intent (NOI) in accordance with the permit requirements." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit NOI submission through AEPACS is the electronic portal. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits *Operational implication:* For each new Mobile County construction site meeting the acreage threshold, a site-specific NOI must be filed electronically via AEPACS before discharge begins.

## Construction Best Management Practices Plan (CBMPP)

"Operators / owners of all regulated construction sites must implement and maintain effective erosion and sediment controls in accordance with a Construction Best Management Practices Plan (CBMPP) prepared and certified by a Qualified Credentialed Professional (QCP)." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit

*Operational implication:* Each permitted site requires its own CBMPP, prepared by a QCP, implemented on-site, and maintained throughout the active construction period. For a workspace managing multiple Mobile County sites, this creates a per-site QCP engagement and CBMPP maintenance obligation.

## QCP Certification

The CBMPP preparation and certification function is assigned to a Qualified Credentialed Professional. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit The CGP program page references a "QCI Program" (Qualified Credentialed Inspector) as a distinct element of the compliance structure. *Operational implication:* The workspace must confirm which of its personnel or contracted professionals hold current QCP/QCI credentials, particularly given that the 2026 permit may have updated credentialing requirements.

*Specific QCP/QCI credentialing criteria under the 2026 permit not available from primary sources as of 2025-07-14.*

## Electronic Reporting

Notices of Intent and other reporting covered by ADEM Admin. Code r. 335-6-1-.04 are subject to the electronic submission requirement effective December 21, 2020. "The applicable signatory and certification requirements identified in 40 CFR part 3 (2016) and rule 335-6-5-.14 or 335-6-6-.09 shall apply to the electronic submission of the reports, notices, and certifications specified in paragraph (2) of this rule." *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits

## Mobile County MS4 — Construction Site Runoff Control

Mobile County's MS4 obligations include: "Controlling stormwater discharges from new development and redevelopment areas (inspections and site plan process)" as one of the requirements of the Phase II permit. The MS4 minimum control measures include "Construction Site Runoff Control." *Source: Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, accessed 2025. https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/ *Operational implication:* Mobile County may inspect construction sites and enforce stormwater controls independently of ADEM inspections. Workspaces should anticipate dual-agency oversight on active sites.

---

# Product-Specific Compliance Status

*No content for this section as of 2025-07-14: the CGP is a discharge/activity permit, not a product regulation. No workspace products are in scope.*

---

# Registration and Reporting Obligations

## NOI Electronic Filing via AEPACS

The NOI for CGP ALR100000 uses ADEM Form 024. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits Submission is through AEPACS. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits *Operational implication:* Each Mobile County construction site at or above the one-acre threshold (or part of a qualifying common plan) requires a separate electronic NOI filing before commencement of land-disturbing activity.

## Notice of Termination (NOT)

*Specific Notice of Termination requirements and process not available from primary sources as of 2025-07-14.* The existence of a NOT process is standard NPDES practice for general permits; the specific 2026 CGP NOT requirements should be confirmed from the permit text.

## MS4 Annual Reporting — Mobile County

Mobile County publishes annual MS4 reports including construction site runoff control activity. The most recent report listed in the source block is the "2023-2024 Municipal Storm Sewer System (MS4) Annual Report." *Source: Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, accessed 2025. https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/ *Operational implication:* Workspaces may be referenced in Mobile County's annual report data if inspections or enforcement actions are recorded. This creates a reputational compliance record beyond ADEM's own files.

## Fee Information

Fee information is accessible through ADEM's Division 1 Regulations, Fee Schedule D. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits *Specific current CGP NOI fee amount not available from primary sources as of 2025-07-14.*

---

# Operational System Requirements

## Per-Site NOI Management System

**Scope:** Each active or planned Mobile County construction site at or above threshold.
**Requirement:** Electronic NOI filed through AEPACS before land disturbance begins; ADEM Form 024 for CGP ALR100000. *Source: ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits
**Gap from current baseline:** For workspaces managing multiple sites, a site registry confirming AEPACS submission status, NOI tracking number, and coverage date for each site is the baseline operational requirement. *Analytical inference:* Workspaces without a centralized permit-tracking system face elevated risk of operating a site without confirmed coverage, particularly when multiple sites are in different phases of construction simultaneously.

## CBMPP Maintenance Per Site

**Scope:** Each permitted site throughout the active construction period.
**Requirement:** A current QCP-certified CBMPP implemented and maintained at each site. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit
**Gap from current baseline:** Workspaces without a CBMPP version-control system risk operating sites under outdated plans, particularly where site conditions change during the project. *Operational implication:* For multi-site operations, a CBMPP currency log (site, QCP name, certification date, last review date) is the minimum tracking infrastructure.

## QCP/QCI Credential Register

**Scope:** Personnel or contractors performing CBMPP preparation and certification functions.
**Requirement:** Credentials must be current at the QCP/QCI level. *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit
**Gap from current baseline:** Workspaces that relied on 2021-era QCP credentials should confirm those credentials remain valid under the 2026 permit framework.

## ADEM Mobile Region Contact — Compliance Oversight

The ADEM Stormwater Contact page confirms regional field office inspector coverage for the Mobile region and provides the general stormwater branch contact: ADEM Water Division, Stormwater Management Branch, Post Office Box 301463, Montgomery, Alabama 36130-1463, (334) 394-4372, cswmail@adem.alabama.gov. *Source: ADEM Stormwater Contact Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit/what-construction-general-permit-cgp/stormwater-contact *Operational implication:* For permit questions specific to Mobile County sites, workspaces should direct inquiries to the Stormwater Management Branch and identify the Mobile-region field staff through the CSW Staff Map linked on the same page.

---

# Exemptions and Edge Cases

## Activities Below One Acre — Common Plan Rule

Construction activity disturbing less than one acre is not subject to CGP coverage unless it is "part of a common plan of development or sale equal to or greater than one acre." *Source: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit *Operational implication:* For workspaces phasing construction across Mobile County parcels, even a small disturbance on an otherwise sub-threshold parcel may trigger CGP coverage if it is planned in conjunction with larger disturbing activities on the same development.

## Forestry Activities

The ADEM water programs include a separate "Forestry BMP Program" distinct from the construction stormwater program. *Source: ADEM Home Page, ADEM, accessed 2025. https://adem.alabama.gov/ *Analytical inference:* Land-clearing activities that are genuinely forestry operations rather than construction may fall under a different regulatory track; however, *Legal Confirmation Required:* whether specific Mobile County site-clearing activities qualify as forestry or construction for CGP purposes requires fact-specific analysis.

---

# Adjacent Industry Research and Alternatives

*No content for this section as of 2025-07-14: no sourced research findings on alternative compliance approaches or adjacent technology relevant to Alabama CGP compliance were available in the source blocks provided.*

---

# Confirmed Regulatory Timeline

| Date | Event | Status |
|---|---|---|
| April 1, 2011 | ADEM General NPDES Permit No. ALR100000 originally established | In force as of that date |
| December 21, 2020 | Electronic NOI submission requirement effective under ADEM Admin. Code r. 335-6-1-.04 | In force as of that date |
| October 1, 2021 | Mobile County Phase II MS4 NPDES permit (ALR040000) effective | In force as of that date |
| April 1, 2026 | 2026 CGP ALR100000 took effect; 2021 CGP expired | In force as of that date |
| September 30, 2026 | Mobile County Phase II MS4 NPDES permit (ALR040000) scheduled to expire | Pending renewal |
| March 31, 2031 | CGP ALR100000 scheduled to expire | Future |

*Sources: ADEM Construction General Permit Program Page, ADEM, https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/construction-general-permit; ADEM NPDES Permits Page, ADEM, https://adem.alabama.gov/water/npdes-programs/npdes-permits; Mobile County Stormwater Management Program Plan, Mobile County, https://www.mobilecountyal.gov/departments/stormwater/

**Required slot — penalty/enforcement provision:** *Specific penalty amounts or enforcement provisions under CGP ALR100000 not available from primary sources as of 2025-07-14.* The NPDES program generally authorizes civil and administrative penalties under the Clean Water Act for unpermitted discharges and permit violations; the specific penalty schedule applicable under the Alabama NPDES program requires retrieval from the permit text and ADEM Admin. Code. *Legal Confirmation Required:* the specific civil penalty exposure for unpermitted discharge or CBMPP non-compliance under the 2026 CGP and applicable Alabama enforcement provisions.

prior.what_it_changes: April 2026 CGP transition forces per-site NOI re-audit and CBMPP currency check across all active Mobile County construction sites immediately.
